content:
2 min read
Just now

--

or a weirdly personal and seemingly chaotic run-up to the potential of saving your money and time — and hopefully preserving the wellbeing of your nervous system
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Damn, everything moves so fast! I just can’t shake this feeling lately, which is unusual, as most of the time I’m not sure what year it is and need good few seconds to figure out the answer for “How old are you?”.
Some things happen way too slow, making others seem barely a flash.
Are becoming an ayahuasca shaman or taking up hobby-horsing really the only ways out?
Oh my dearest boi.
Never in my wildest dreams had I foreseen that playing Neverwinter Nights when I was twelve would lead me to the front of the queue to experience digital existential dread — still fresh, in its default temperature, straight out of wherever it dwells in its spare time (beg my pardon sir, in what time?).
WILD.
But then again, I so much enjoyed Neverwinter along with all the times and spaces it led me to… Dread, my long awaited friend, I welcome you with arms wide open!
Anyway, that crazy pace is what brought us all here — in attempt to avoid losing myself in perpetual state of wonder I automated the automation. If keeping up is impossible, maybe at least tools can be controlled not to wriggle out of one’s arms while they cultivate sense of your absolute correctness with their newly found voice. May it be, you recon?
Out of all this circular motion comes the project I would love to share with you — ai-dotfiles.
It is a configuration engine for AI coding environments. One command installs rules, skills, agents, prompts, and MCP servers into Cursor or VS Code + Copilot — the same setup, reproduced across every project and every machine. It’s a countermeasure layer for the four taxes of an unmanaged coding agent:

• Tokens — terse-mode rules and model tiering, ~75% output reduction

• Mistakes — execution-layer hooks that block destructive git commands even when the model ignores instructions

• Amnesia — automatic session handoff and resume across chats and model switches

• Drift — validated, reproducible installs across machines and teammates
Clone it, run setup.sh, and tell me what a bliss your new AI friend is :glitter: :glitter: :explosion:
